text: The surgeon retracts the transverse mesocolon caudally to expose the hernia orifice located on the left side of the duodenal-jejunal junction. The incarcerated small bowel loops are carefully reduced into the peritoneal cavity, extending just beyond the fourth part of the duodenum. No signs of small bowel ischemia are observed.

The surgeon evaluates the hernia sac, observing a small amount of serohemorrhagic fluid. Using a laparoscopic suturing device, a non-absorbable suture is applied in a purse string method to close the hernia orifice, taking serosal bites from the jejunal segment.
